nav.Dashboard.what: Your at-a-glance overview — NCAT readiness, money in and out, and which months still need statements.

nav.Upload.what: Add ANZ statement PDFs; the app reads every transaction automatically.

nav.Identity.what: Who's who — Ron's details, your details as manager, and the significant people (Plan Section A).

nav.Inventory.what: Everything Ron owns and owes — accounts, property, investments, vehicles, bonds, debts (Plan Sections B–C).

nav.Forecast.what: The 12-month income and spending plan (Plan Section D).

nav.Gifts.what: The gift ledger — planned vs actually given, with §76 limit flags.

nav.One-off Events.what: Large unusual transactions — confirm real one-offs or dismiss them (Plan Section E).

nav.Consultations.what: Your record of consulting Ron and the family on big decisions (Plan Section F).

nav.Submissions.what: Generate the NSWTG forms — Annual Accounts and the Plan — and see what's missing first.

nav.Compliance.what: The rule book on a switchboard — warnings, blocks, and early warnings before they become problems.

nav.Transactions.what: Every bank transaction — browse, search, and fix categories.

nav.Export.what: Download the monthly Excel budget files.

dashboard.what: Your home page. It shows how ready you are for NSW Trustee & Guardian reporting, the headline money numbers, and whether any bank statements are missing.
dashboard.source: Everything here is calculated live from the transactions and estate details stored in this app — nothing is typed in by hand.
dashboard.do: Glance at the NCAT readiness strip first. If anything is red or yellow, the quick-link buttons take you straight to the page that fixes it.

dashboard.ncat_readiness.what: Five gauges for your reporting obligations: compliance blocks (must fix before submitting), warnings (worth reviewing), how complete each of the two NSWTG forms is, and gifts that may breach the gift rules.
dashboard.ncat_readiness.source: The compliance engine checks the NSWTG handbook rules against the live data; the two 'ready' percentages come from the same audit the Submissions page uses.
dashboard.ncat_readiness.do: Aim for 0 blocks and rising 'ready' percentages. Use the buttons below the gauges to jump straight to Compliance, Submissions, or Gifts.

dashboard.month_status.what: A grid of the 13 statement months (Jun 2025 – Jun 2026) for each of Ron's three ANZ accounts. ✓ means that month's statement is loaded; ⚠ means it is missing.
dashboard.month_status.source: Coverage is worked out from the statement PDFs you have uploaded.
dashboard.month_status.do: If a month shows ⚠, find that statement and add it on the Upload page. One quirk to remember: the Spending card only existed from August 2025, so earlier ⚠ marks for it are not real gaps.

dashboard.charts.what: Four pictures of the money story: monthly spending vs income, where the money goes by category, the running net position, and the top five spending categories.
dashboard.charts.source: Drawn from the categorised bank transactions. The 'View' picker above filters the category charts to a single month; the trend lines always show the full year.
dashboard.charts.do: Use these to spot odd months before NSWTG does — a spike worth explaining often becomes a one-off event or a note in the accounts.

dashboard.view_filter.what: Filter the totals and category charts to a single month, or keep the whole year in view.

dashboard.months_complete.what: How many of the 13 fiscal months have statements loaded for all three accounts.
